ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chicken gizzard as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for poultry organ meats is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of raw chicken gizzard is approximately 85 g, equivalent to two to three medium gizzards before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, gizzard, all classes, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05028
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for raw chicken gizzard (cr_id F11010027), which is an approved primary regional source. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05028 (Chicken, gizzard, all classes, raw) is cited as the approved secondary source for fatty acid fractions (saturated fat 0.55 g/100 g, monounsaturated fat 0.44 g/100 g, polyunsaturated fat 0.46 g/100 g) not separately reported by HPB. Both HPB and USDA FoodData Central are approved sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05028 and HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database — not applicable; carbohydrate content is 0.0 g/100 g
gi_source.source_match_notes: Chicken gizzard, raw; glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g confirmed by both HPB Singapore Food Composition Database and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05028). The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted and does not list a GI value for chicken gizzard, consistent with the expectation that pure protein and organ meat foods with zero carbohydrate content are not assigned a GI value. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (69 mg/100 g) with a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio (potassium 237 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Raw chicken gizzard is suitable for hypertensive individuals in its uncooked state due to very low intrinsic sodium and a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH diet principles; however, cooking with soy sauce, oyster sauce, or added salt will substantially raise sodium content and should be avoided or minimised using low-sodium alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein (17.66 g/100 g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chicken gizzard, raw, contains no carbohydrate or sugar and therefore has no glycaemic impact, making it an appropriate protein source for individuals managing type 2 diabetes; preparation methods that add sugary sauces (hoisin, teriyaki, sweet soy) must be avoided to preserve this benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High dietary cholesterol (240 mg/100 g) warrants moderation; low saturated fat (0.55 g/100 g) and low sodium are favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While raw chicken gizzard is low in saturated fat and sodium, its high cholesterol content (240 mg/100 g) means individuals with established cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia should limit portion size and frequency, and avoid high-fat cooking methods such as deep-frying that would further increase the atherogenic load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken gizzard, raw, is the uncooked muscular stomach organ of the chicken, responsible for grinding food in the digestive system. It is a dense, firm, dark-red organ meat with a chewy texture and a mildly gamey, savoury flavour. Raw chicken gizzard is widely used across Asian cuisines — including Chinese, Thai, Vietnamese, and Singaporean cooking — as well as in Latin American and African dishes, typically after thorough cleaning and trimming of the inner lining. Nutritionally, raw chicken gizzard is a high-protein, very low-fat food providing 94 kcal, 17.66 g protein, and only 2.06 g fat per 100 g, with zero carbohydrate and zero sugar. It is an excellent source of iron (2.49 mg/100 g) and provides meaningful potassium (237 mg/100 g) and calcium (11 mg/100 g). Sodium is very low at 69 mg/100 g in the raw state. The food is notable for its high cholesterol content (240 mg/100 g), which warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular risk. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, and is halal when sourced from appropriately slaughtered poultry. Clinically, it is suitable for type 2 diabetes management due to zero glycaemic impact, and for hypertension management due to low sodium and a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio. Cardiovascular suitability is moderate given the high cholesterol content. Common preparations include stir-frying, braising, skewering (yakitori-style), and inclusion in soups and congee.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id F11010027, confidence 0.95), which is an approved primary regional source. Fatty acid fractions supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05028 (chicken gizzard, all classes, raw), also an approved secondary source under Project Veritas v2. GI is not applicable due to zero carbohydrate content; University of Sydney GI Database consulted and confirms no GI value is assigned for this food.
notes: carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database — raw chicken gizzard contains no digestible carbohydrate. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB — organ meat contains no dietary fibre. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible for raw poultry organ meat per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05028). Saturated fat (0.55 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (0.44 g/100 g), and polyunsaturated fat (0.46 g/100 g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05028 as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions separately for this entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for this food item; while USDA FoodData Central does report some of these values for chicken gizzard, they have not been incorporated in this record version to avoid unvalidated cross-source mixing without explicit provenance tracking. This record covers the raw, uncooked state only; cooked preparations will have substantially different nutrient profiles, particularly for sodium if seasoned. Halal status applies when sourced from appropriately certified poultry. This food is a generic international ingredient and not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory local-language naming requirements apply beyond English. GI not applicable: chicken gizzard, raw, contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g; glycemic index is naturally zero-carbohydrate food and GI is not assigned.
